# Malicious Software — Question Bank

```yaml
id: mal-m01
topic: malicious_software
type: mc
points: 2
section: II
difficulty: 1
bloom: understand
exam-eligible: true
tags: [used/su26]
stem: Which statement best distinguishes a worm from a virus?
outcomes:
  - {key: a, text: "A worm attaches to a host; a virus spreads independently", credited: false, feedback: "Reversed — the worm is the self-propagating one."}
  - {key: b, text: "A worm self-propagates over the network with no host/user; a virus needs a host trigger", credited: true,  feedback: "Correct. Propagation is the defining axis."}
  - {key: c, text: "A worm encrypts; a virus steals credentials", credited: false, feedback: "Conflates payload with propagation."}
  - {key: d, text: "No technical difference", credited: false, feedback: "There is a clear propagation distinction."}
  - {key: none, text: "No answer / multiple marks", credited: false, feedback: ""}
citation: Stallings & Brown 6.3
```
